giddy with the fashion too, that thou hast shifted out
of thy tale into telling me of the fashion?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text:
Not so, neither. But know that I have tonight
wooed Margaret, the Lady Hero’s gentlewoman, by the name
of Hero. She leans me out at her mistress' chamber
window, bids me a thousand times good night. I tell this
tale vilely. I should first tell thee how the Prince,
Claudio and my master, planted and placed and possessed
by my master Don John, saw afar off in the orchard this
amiable encounter.

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: And thought they Margaret was Hero?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text:
Two of them did, the Prince and Claudio, but the devil
my master knew she was Margaret; and partly by his
oaths, which first possessed them, partly by the dark
night, which did deceive them, but chiefly by my
villainy, which did confirm any slander that Don John
had made, away went Claudio enraged, swore he would meet
her as he was appointed next morning at the temple, and
there, before the whole congregation, shame her with
what he saw o'ernight
and send her home again without a husband.

units.speaker: SECOND WATCHMAN
units.text: We charge you, in the Prince’s name, stand!

units.speaker: FIRST WATCHMAN
units.text:
Call up the right Master Constable. We have
here recovered the most dangerous piece of lechery that
ever was known in the commonwealth.

units.speaker: SECOND WATCHMAN
units.text:
And one Deformed is one of them. I know him; he wears
a lock.

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: Masters, masters—

units.speaker: SECOND WATCHMAN
units.text:
[to BORACHIO] You’ll be made bring Deformed forth,
I warrant you.

units.speaker: FIRST WATCHMAN
units.text:
Masters, never speak, we charge you, let us obey you
go with us.

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text:
We are like to prove a goodly commodity, being taken up
of these men’s bills.

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text:
A commodity in question, I warrant you.—Come,
we’ll obey you.
Exeunt
Enter HERO, MARGARET, and URSULA

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
Good Ursula, wake my cousin Beatrice and desire her
to rise.

units.speaker: URSULA
units.text: I will, lady.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: And bid her come hither.

units.speaker: URSULA
units.text: Well.
Exit

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: Troth, I think your other rebato were better.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: No, pray thee, good Meg, I’ll wear this.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
By my troth, ’s not so good, and I warrant your cousin
will say so.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
My cousin’s a fool, and thou art another. I’ll wear
none but this.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
I like the new tire within excellently, if the hair
were a thought browner; and your gown’s a most rare
fashion, i' faith. I saw the Duchess of Milan’s gown
that they praise so.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Oh, that exceeds, they say.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
By my troth, ’s but a nightgown in respect of yours
—cloth o' gold, and cuts, and laced with silver, set with
pearls, down sleeves, side sleeves, and skirts, round
underborne with a bluish tinsel. But for a fine, quaint,
graceful, and excellent fashion, yours is worth ten on
’t.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
God give me joy to wear it, for my heart is exceeding
heavy.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: 'Twill be heavier soon by the weight of a man.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Fie upon thee! Art not ashamed?

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Of what, lady? Of speaking honorably? Is not
marriage honorable in a beggar? Is not your lord
honorable without marriage? I think you would have me
say, “Saving your reverence, a husband.” An bad thinking
do not wrest true speaking, I’ll offend nobody. Is
there any harm in “the heavier for a husband?” None, I
think, an it be the right husband and the right wife.
Otherwise, ’tis light and not heavy. Ask my Lady Beatrice
else. Here she comes.
Enter BEATRICE

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Good morrow, coz.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Good morrow, sweet Hero.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Why, how now? Do you speak in the sick tune?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: I am out of all other tune, methinks.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Clap ’s into “Light o' love.” That goes without a
burden. Do you sing it, and I’ll dance it.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Ye light o' love, with your heels! Then, if your
husband have stables enough, you’ll see he shall lack no
barns.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
O illegitimate construction! I scorn that with my
heels.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
'Tis almost five o'clock, cousin. 'Tis time you were
ready. By my troth, I am exceeding ill. Heigh-ho!

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: For a hawk, a horse, or a husband?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: For the letter that begins them all, H.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Well, an you be not turned Turk, there’s no more
sailing by the star.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: What means the fool, trow?

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Nothing, I; but God send everyone their heart’s
desire.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
These gloves the Count sent me, they are an
excellent perfume.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: I am stuffed, cousin. I cannot smell.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: A maid, and stuffed! There’s goodly catching of cold.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Oh, God help me, God help me! How long have
you professed apprehension?

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Even since you left it. Doth not my wit become me
rarely?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
It is not seen enough; you should wear it in your cap.
By my troth, I am sick.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Get you some of this distilled carduus benedictus and
lay it to your heart. It is the only thing for a qualm.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: There thou prick’st her with a thistle.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Benedictus! Why benedictus? You have some moral in
this benedictus?

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Moral! No, by my troth, I have no moral meaning. I
meant plain holy thistle. You may think perchance that I
think you are in love. Nay, by 'r Lady, I am not such a
fool to think what I list, nor I list not to think what
I can, nor indeed I cannot think, if I would think my
heart out of thinking, that you are in love or that you
will be in love or that you can be in love. Yet Benedick
was such another, and now is he become a man. He swore
he would never marry, and yet now, in despite of his
heart, he eats his meat without grudging. And how you
may be converted I know not, but methinks you look with
your eyes as other women do.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: What pace is this that thy tongue keeps?

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: Not a false gallop.
Enter URSULA

units.speaker: URSULA
units.text:
Madam, withdraw: the Prince, the Count,
Signor Benedick, Don John, and all the gallants of the
town are come to fetch you to church.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Help to dress me, good coz, good Meg, good Ursula.
Exeunt
Enter LEONATO with DOGBERRY and VERGES

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: What would you with me, honest neighbor?

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Marry, sir, I would have some confidence with you
that decerns you nearly.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Brief, I pray you, for you see it is a busy time with
me.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Marry, this it is, sir.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: Yes, in truth it is, sir.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: What is it, my good friends?

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Goodman Verges, sir, speaks a little off the matter. An
old man, sir, and his wits are not so blunt as, God
help, I would desire they were, but, in faith, honest as
the skin between his brows.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text:
Yes, I thank God I am as honest as any man living that
is an old man and no honester than I.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Comparisons are odorous. Palabras , neighbor Verges.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Neighbors, you are tedious.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
It pleases your Worship to say so, but we are the poor
duke’s officers. But truly, for mine own part, if I were
as tedious as a king, I could find it in my heart to
bestow it all of your worship.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: All thy tediousness on me, ah?

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Yea, an ’twere a thousand pound more than ’tis, for I
hear as good exclamation on your Worship as of any man
in the city, and though I be but a poor man, I am glad
to hear it.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: And so am I.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I would fain know what you have to say.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text:
Marry, sir, our watch tonight, excepting your
Worship’s presence, ha' ta'en a couple of as arrant
knaves as any in Messina.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
A good old man, sir. He will be talking. As they say,
“When the age is in, the wit is out.” God help us, it is
a world to see! Well said, i' faith, neighbor Verges.
—Well, God’s a good man. An two men ride of a horse, one
must ride behind. An honest soul, i' faith, sir, by my
troth he is, as ever broke bread, but God is to be
worshipped, all men are not alike, alas, good neighbor!

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Indeed, neighbor, he comes too short of you.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Gifts that God gives.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I must leave you.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
One word, sir. Our watch, sir, have indeed
comprehended two aspicious persons, and we would have
them this morning examined before your worship.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Take their examination yourself and bring it me. I am
now in great haste, as it may appear unto you.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: It shall be suffigance.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Drink some wine ere you go. Fare you well.
Enter a MESSENGER

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
My lord, they stay for you to give your daughter to
her husband.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I’ll wait upon them. I am ready.
Exeunt LEONATO and MESSENGER

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Go, good partner, go, get you to Francis Seacole. Bid
him bring his pen and inkhorn to the jail. We are now
to examination these men.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: And we must do it wisely.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
We will spare for no wit, I warrant you. Here’s that
shall drive some of them to a noncome. Only get the
learned writer to set down our excommunication and meet
me at the jail.
Exeunt
Enter DON PEDRO, DON JOHN, LEONATO, FRIAR FRANCIS, CLAUDIO, BENEDICK, HERO, BEATRICE, and Attendants

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Come, Friar Francis, be brief, only to the plain form
of marriage, and you shall recount their particular
duties afterwards.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
[to CLAUDIO] You come hither, my lord, to marry this
lady?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: No.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: To be married to her.—Friar, you come to marry her.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: Lady, you come hither to be married to this count?

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: I do.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
If either of you know any inward impediment why
you should not be conjoined, charge you on your souls to
utter it.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Know you any, Hero?

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: None, my lord.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: Know you any, count?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I dare make his answer, none.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
O, what men dare do! What men may do! What men
daily do, not knowing what they do!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
How now, interjections? Why, then, some be of
laughing, as, ah, ha, he!

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Stand thee by, Friar.—Father, by your leave, Will you
with free and unconstrainèd soul
Give me this maid, your daughter?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: As freely, son, as God did give her me.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
And what have I to give you back whose worth May
counterpoise this rich and precious gift?

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Nothing, unless you render her again.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Sweet Prince, you learn me noble thankfulness.—
There, Leonato, take her back again.
Give not this rotten orange to your friend.
She’s but the sign and semblance of her honor.
Behold how like a maid she blushes here!
Oh, what authority and show of truth
Can cunning sin cover itself withal!
Comes not that blood as modest evidence
To witness simple virtue? Would you not swear,
All you that see her, that she were a maid
By these exterior shows? But she is none.
She knows the heat of a luxurious bed.
Her blush is guiltiness, not modesty.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: What do you mean, my lord?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Not to be married,
Not to knit my soul to an approvèd wanton.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Dear my lord, if you in your own proof
Have vanquished the resistance of her youth
And made defeat of her virginity—

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
I know what you would say: if I have known her,
You will say she did embrace me as a husband,
And so extenuate the forehand sin. No, Leonato,
I never tempted her with word too large
But, as a brother to his sister, showed
Bashful sincerity and comely love.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: And seemed I ever otherwise to you?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Out on thee, seeming! I will write against it.
You seem to me as Dian in her orb,
As chaste as is the bud ere it be blown.
But you are more intemperate in your blood
Than Venus, or those pampered animals
That rage in savage sensuality.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: Is my lord well, that he doth speak so wide?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Sweet Prince, why speak not you?

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
What should I speak?
I stand dishonored, that have gone about
To link my dear friend to a common stale.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Are these things spoken, or do I but dream?

units.speaker: DON JOHN
units.text: Sir, they are spoken, and these things are true.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: This looks not like a nuptial.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: True! O God!

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Leonato, stand I here?
Is this the Prince? Is this the Prince’s brother?
Is this face Hero’s? Are our eyes our own?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: All this is so, but what of this, my lord?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Let me but move one question to your daughter,
And by that fatherly and kindly power
That you have in her, bid her answer truly.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I charge thee do so, as thou art my child.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
Oh, God defend me! how am I beset!—
What kind of catechizing call you this?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: To make you answer truly to your name.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
Is it not Hero? Who can blot that name
With any just reproach?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Marry, that can Hero!
Hero itself can blot out Hero’s virtue.
What man was he talked with you yesternight
Out at your window betwixt twelve and one?
Now, if you are a maid, answer to this.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text: I talked with no man at that hour, my lord.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Why, then are you no maiden. —Leonato,
I am sorry you must hear. Upon mine honor,
Myself, my brother, and this grievèd count
Did see her, hear her, at that hour last night
Talk with a ruffian at her chamber window
Who hath indeed, most like a liberal villain,
Confessed the vile encounters they have had
A thousand times in secret.

units.speaker: DON JOHN
units.text:
Fie, fie, they are not to be named, my lord,
Not to be spoke of!
There is not chastity enough in language,
Without offense, to utter them. —Thus, pretty lady,
I am sorry for thy much misgovernment.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
O Hero, what a Hero hadst thou been
If half thy outward graces had been placed
About thy thoughts and counsels of thy heart!
But fare thee well, most foul, most fair! Farewell,
Thou pure impiety and impious purity.
For thee I’ll lock up all the gates of love,
And on my eyelids shall conjecture hang,
To turn all beauty into thoughts of harm,
And never shall it more be gracious.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Hath no man’s dagger here a point for me?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Why, how now, cousin! wherefore sink you down?

units.speaker: DON JOHN
units.text:
Come, let us go. These things, come thus to light,
Smother her spirits up.
Exeunt DON PEDRO, DON JOHN, and CLAUDIO

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: How doth the lady?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Dead, I think.—Help, uncle!—
Hero, why, Hero! Uncle! Signor Benedick! Friar!

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
O Fate! Take not away thy heavy hand!
Death is the fairest cover for her shame
That may be wished for.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: How now, cousin Hero!

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: [to HERO] Have comfort, lady.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: [to HERO] Dost thou look up?

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: Yea, wherefore should she not?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Wherefore! Why, doth not every earthly thing
Cry shame upon her? Could she here deny
The story that is printed in her blood?—
Do not live, Hero, do not ope thine eyes,
For, did I think thou wouldst not quickly die,
Thought I thy spirits were stronger than thy shames,
Myself would, on the rearward of reproaches,
Strike at thy life. Grieved I I had but one?
Chid I for that at frugal Nature’s frame?
O, one too much by thee! Why had I one?
Why ever wast thou lovely in my eyes?
Why had I not with charitable hand
Took up a beggar’s issue at my gates,
Who, smirchèd thus, and mired with infamy,
I might have said, “No part of it is mine;
This shame derives itself from unknown loins”?
But mine, and mine I loved, and mine I praised,
And mine that I was proud on, mine so much
That I myself was to myself not mine,
Valuing of her– why, she, O she is fall'n
Into a pit of ink, that the wide sea
Hath drops too few to wash her clean again
And salt too little which may season give
To her foul tainted flesh!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Sir, sir, be patient.
For my part, I am so attired in wonder
I know not what to say.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Oh, on my soul, my cousin is belied!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Lady, were you her bedfellow last night?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
No, truly not, although until last night
I have this twelvemonth been her bedfellow.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Confirmed, confirmed! Oh, that is stronger made
Which was before barred up with ribs of iron!
Would the two princes lie and Claudio lie,
Who loved her so that, speaking of her foulness,
Washed it with tears? Hence from her. Let her die.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
Hear me a little,
For I have only silent been so long,
And given way unto this course of fortune,
By noting of the lady. I have marked
A thousand blushing apparitions
To start into her face, a thousand innocent shames
In angel whiteness beat away those blushes,
And in her eye there hath appeared a fire
To burn the errors that these princes hold
Against her maiden truth. Call me a fool,
Trust not my reading nor my observations,
Which with experimental seal doth warrant
The tenor of my book; trust not my age,
My reverence, calling, nor divinity,
If this sweet lady lie not guiltless here
Under some biting error.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Friar, it cannot be.
Thou seest that all the grace that she hath left
Is that she will not add to her damnation
A sin of perjury. She not denies it.
Why seek’st thou then to cover with excuse
That which appears in proper nakedness?

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: Lady, what man is he you are accused of?

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
They know that do accuse me. I know none.
If I know more of any man alive
Than that which maiden modesty doth warrant,
Let all my sins lack mercy! —O my father,
Prove you that any man with me conversed
At hours unmeet, or that I yesternight
Maintained the change of words with any creature,
Refuse me, hate me, torture me to death!

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: There is some strange misprision in the princes.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Two of them have the very bent of honor,
And if their wisdoms be misled in this,
The practice of it lives in John the Bastard,
Whose spirits toil in frame of villainies.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
I know not. If they speak but truth of her,
These hands shall tear her; if they wrong her honor,
The proudest of them shall well hear of it.
Time hath not yet so dried this blood of mine
Nor age so eat up my invention
Nor fortune made such havoc of my means
Nor my bad life reft me so much of friends
But they shall find, awaked in such a kind,
Both strength of limb and policy of mind,
Ability in means and choice of friends,
To quit me of them throughly.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
Pause awhile,
And let my counsel sway you in this case.
Your daughter here the princes left for dead.
Let her awhile be secretly kept in
And publish it that she is dead indeed.
Maintain a mourning ostentation,
And on your family’s old monument
Hang mournful epitaphs and do all rites
That appertain unto a burial.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: What shall become of this? What will this do?

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
Marry, this, well carried, shall on her behalf
Change slander to remorse. That is some good.
But not for that dream I on this strange course,
But on this travail look for greater birth.
She, dying, as it must so be maintained,
Upon the instant that she was accused,
Shall be lamented, pitied and excused
Of every hearer. For it so falls out
That what we have we prize not to the worth
Whiles we enjoy it, but being lacked and lost,
Why then we rack the value, then we find
The virtue that possession would not show us
Whiles it was ours. So will it fare with Claudio.
When he shall hear she died upon his words,
The idea of her life shall sweetly creep
Into his study of imagination,
And every lovely organ of her life
Shall come apparelled in more precious habit,
More moving, delicate and full of life,
Into the eye and prospect of his soul
Than when she lived indeed. Then shall he mourn,
If ever love had interest in his liver,
And wish he had not so accused her,
No, though he thought his accusation true.
Let this be so, and doubt not but success
Will fashion the event in better shape
Than I can lay it down in likelihood.
But if all aim but this be leveled false,
The supposition of the lady's death
Will quench the wonder of her infamy.
And if it sort not well, you may conceal her,
As best befits her wounded reputation,
In some reclusive and religious life,
Out of all eyes, tongues, minds, and injuries.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Signior Leonato, let the friar advise you.
And though you know my inwardness and love
Is very much unto the Prince and Claudio,
Yet, by mine honor, I will deal in this
As secretly and justly as your soul
Should with your body.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Being that I flow in grief,
The smallest twine may lead me.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
'Tis well consented. Presently away,
For to strange sores strangely they strain the cure.—
Come, lady, die to live. This wedding day
Perhaps is but prolonged. Have patience and endure.
Exeunt all but BENEDICK and BEATRICE

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Lady Beatrice, have you wept all this while?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Yea, and I will weep a while longer.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: I will not desire that.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: You have no reason. I do it freely.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Surely I do believe your fair cousin is wronged.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Ah, how much might the man deserve of me that
would right her!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Is there any way to show such friendship?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: A very even way, but no such friend.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: May a man do it?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: It is a man’s office, but not yours.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
I do love nothing in the world so well as you. Is not
that strange?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
As strange as the thing I know not. It were as possible
for me to say I loved nothing so well as you, but
believe me not, and yet I lie not, I confess nothing,
nor I deny nothing. I am sorry for my cousin.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: By my sword, Beatrice, thou lovest me.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Do not swear, and eat it.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
I will swear by it that you love me, and I will make
him eat it that says I love not you.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Will you not eat your word?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
With no sauce that can be devised to it. I protest I
love thee.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Why then, God forgive me.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: What offense, sweet Beatrice?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
You have stayed me in a happy hour. I was about to
protest
I loved you.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: And do it with all thy heart.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
I love you with so much of my heart that none is left
to protest.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Come, bid me do anything for thee.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Kill Claudio.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Ha! Not for the wide world.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: You kill me to deny it. Farewell.
BEATRICE begins to exit

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Tarry, sweet Beatrice.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
I am gone, though I am here. There is no love in you.
Nay, I pray you let me go.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Beatrice—

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: In faith, I will go.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: We’ll be friends first.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
You dare easier be friends with me than fight with
mine enemy.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Is Claudio thine enemy?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Is he not approved in the height a villain, that
hath slandered, scorned, dishonored my kinswoman? Oh,
that I were a man! What, bear her in hand until they
come to take hands and then, with public accusation,
uncovered slander, unmitigated rancor —O God, that I were
a man! I would eat his heart in the marketplace.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Hear me, Beatrice—

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Talk with a man out at a window! A proper saying!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Nay, but Beatrice—

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Sweet Hero, she is wronged, she is slandered, she
is undone.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Beat—

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Princes and counties! Surely, a princely testimony, a
goodly count, Count Comfect, a sweet gallant, surely!
Oh, that I were a man for his sake! Or that I had any
friend would be a man for my sake! But manhood is melted
into curtsies, valor into compliment, and men are only
turned into tongue, and trim ones too. He is now as
valiant as Hercules that only tells a lie and swears it.
I cannot be a man with wishing, therefore I will die a
woman with grieving.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Tarry, good Beatrice. By this hand, I love thee.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Use it for my love some other way than swearing by it.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Think you in your soul the Count Claudio hath
wronged Hero?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Yea, as sure as I have a thought or a soul.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Enough, I am engaged. I will challenge him. I will
kiss your hand, and so I leave you. By this hand,
Claudio shall render me a dear account. As you hear of
me, so think of me. Go comfort your cousin. I must say
she is dead, and so, farewell.
Exeunt
Enter DOGBERRY, VERGES, and SEXTON, in gowns; and the Watch, with CONRAD and BORACHIO

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Is our whole dissembly appeared?

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: Oh, a stool and a cushion for the Sexton.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text: Which be the malefactors?

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Marry, that am I and my partner.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text:
Nay, that’s certain; we have the exhibition to
examine.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text:
But which are the offenders that are to be examined?
Let
them come before Master Constable.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Yea, marry, let them come before me.
BORACHIO and CONRAD come forward

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: What is your name, friend?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: Borachio.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Pray, write down, “Borachio.”—Yours, sirrah?

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: I am a gentleman, sir, and my name is Conrad.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Write down “Master Gentleman Conrad.”—Masters, do you
serve God?

units.speaker: CONRAD BORACHIO
units.text: Yea, sir, we hope.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Write down that they hope they serve God; and write
God first, for God defend but God should go before
such villains! —Masters, it is proved already that you
are little better than false knaves, and it will go near
to be thought so shortly. How answer you for
yourselves?

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: Marry, sir, we say we are none.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
A marvelous witty fellow, I assure you, but I will go
about with him.—Come you hither, sirrah, a word in your
ear. Sir, I say to you it is thought you are false
knaves.

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: Sir, I say to you we are none.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Well, stand aside.—'Fore God, they are both in a ale.
Have you writ down that they are none?

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text:
Master Constable, you go not the way to examine.
You must call forth the watch that are their accusers.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Yea, marry, that’s the eftest way.—Let the watch
come forth. Masters, I charge you in the Prince’s name,
accuse these men.

units.speaker: FIRST WATCHMAN
units.text:
This man said, sir, that Don John, the Prince’s
brother, was a villain.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Write down Prince John a villain. Why, this is flat
perjury, to call a prince’s brother villain.

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: Master Constable—

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Pray thee, fellow, peace. I do not like thy look, I
promise thee.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text: [to Watch] What heard you him say else?

units.speaker: SECOND WATCHMAN
units.text:
Marry, that he had received a thousand ducats of Don
John for accusing the Lady Hero wrongfully.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Flat burglary as ever was committed.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: Yea, by Mass, that it is.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text: What else, fellow?

units.speaker: FIRST WATCHMAN
units.text:
And that Count Claudio did mean upon his words
to disgrace Hero before the whole assembly, and not
marry her.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
[to BORACHIO] O villain! Thou wilt be condemned
into everlasting redemption for this.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text: What else?

units.speaker: FIRST WATCHMAN
units.text: This is all.

units.speaker: SEXTON
units.text:
And this is more, masters, than you can deny. Prince
John is this morning secretly stolen away. Hero was in
this manner accused, in this very manner refused, and
upon the grief of this, suddenly died. —Master Constable,
let these men be bound and brought to Leonato’s. I will
go before and show him their examination.
Exit

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: Come, let them be opinioned.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text: Let them be in the hands—

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: Off, coxcomb!

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
God’s my life, where’s the Sexton? Let him write down
the
Prince’s officer “coxcomb.” Come, bind them.—Thou
naughty varlet!

units.speaker: CONRAD
units.text: Away! You are an ass, you are an ass!

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Dost thou not suspect my place? Dost thou not suspect
my years? Oh, that he were here to write me down an ass!
But masters, remember that I am an ass, though it be
not written down, yet forget not that I am an ass. —No,
thou villain, thou art full of piety, as shall be proved
upon thee by good witness. I am a wise fellow and,
which is more, an officer and, which is more, a
householder and, which is more, as pretty a piece of
flesh as any is in Messina, and one that knows the law,
go to, and a rich fellow enough, go to, and a fellow
that hath had losses, and one that hath two gowns and
everything handsome about him. —Bring him away.—Oh, that
I had been writ down an ass!
Exeunt
Enter LEONATO and ANTONIO

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
If you go on thus, you will kill yourself,
And ’tis not wisdom thus to second grief
Against yourself.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
I pray thee, cease thy counsel,
Which falls into mine ears as profitless
As water in a sieve. Give not me counsel,
Nor let no comforter delight mine ear
But such a one whose wrongs do suit with mine.
Bring me a father that so loved his child,
Whose joy of her is overwhelmed like mine,
And bid him speak of patience.
Measure his woe the length and breadth of mine,
And let it answer every strain for strain,
As thus for thus and such a grief for such,
In every lineament, branch, shape, and form.
If such a one will smile and stroke his beard,
Bid sorrow wag, cry “hem” when he should groan,
Patch grief with proverbs, make misfortune drunk
With candle-wasters, bring him yet to me
And I of him will gather patience.
But there is no such man. For, brother, men
Can counsel and speak comfort to that grief
Which they themselves not feel, but, tasting it,
Their counsel turns to passion which before
Would give preceptial med'cine to rage,
Fetter strong madness in a silken thread,
Charm ache with air, and agony with words.
No, no, 'tis all men's office to speak patience
To those that wring under the load of sorrow,
But no man's virtue nor sufficiency
To be so moral when he shall endure
The like himself. Therefore give me no counsel.
My griefs cry louder than advertisement.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Therein do men from children nothing differ.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
I pray thee, peace. I will be flesh and blood,
For there was never yet philosopher
That could endure the toothache patiently,
However they have writ the style of gods
And made a push at chance and sufferance.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Yet bend not all the harm upon yourself.
Make those that do offend you suffer too.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
There thou speak’st reason. Nay, I will do so.
My soul doth tell me Hero is belied,
And that shall Claudio know; so shall the Prince
And all of them that thus dishonor her.
Enter DON PEDRO and CLAUDIO

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Here comes the Prince and Claudio hastily.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Good e'en, good e'en.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Good day to both of you.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Hear you, my lords—

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: We have some haste, Leonato.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Some haste, my lord! Well, fare you well, my lord.
Are you so hasty now? Well, all is one.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Nay, do not quarrel with us, good old man.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
If he could right himself with quarreling,
Some of us would lie low.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Who wrongs him?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Marry, thou dost wrong me, thou dissembler, thou.
Nay, never lay thy hand upon thy sword.
I fear thee not.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Marry, beshrew my hand
If it should give your age such cause of fear.
In faith, my hand meant nothing to my sword.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Tush, tush, man, never fleer and jest at me.
I speak not like a dotard nor a fool,
As under privilege of age to brag
What I have done being young, or what would do
Were I not old. Know, Claudio, to thy head,
Thou hast so wronged mine innocent child and me
That I am forced to lay my reverence by,
And with gray hairs and bruise of many days
Do challenge thee to trial of a man.
I say thou hast belied mine innocent child.
Thy slander hath gone through and through her heart,
And she lies buried with her ancestors,
Oh, in a tomb where never scandal slept
Save this of hers, framed by thy villainy.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: My villainy?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Thine, Claudio, thine, I say.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: You say not right, old man.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
My lord, my lord,
I’ll prove it on his body if he dare,
Despite his nice fence and his active practice,
His May of youth and bloom of lustihood.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Away! I will not have to do with you.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Canst thou so daff me? Thou hast killed my child.
If thou kill’st me, boy, thou shalt kill a man.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
He shall kill two of us, and men indeed,
But that’s no matter. Let him kill one first.
Win me and wear me! Let him answer me.—
Come, follow me, boy. Come, sir boy, come, follow me.
Sir boy, I’ll whip you from your foining fence,
Nay, as I am a gentleman, I will.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Brother—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Content yourself. God knows I loved my niece,
And she is dead, slandered to death by villains
That dare as well answer a man indeed
As I dare take a serpent by the tongue. —
Boys, apes, braggarts, jacks, milksops!

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Brother Anthony—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Hold you content. What, man! I know them, yea,
And what they weigh, even to the utmost scruple —
Scrambling, outfacing, fashion-monging boys,
That lie and cog and flout, deprave and slander,
Go anticly and show outward hideousness,
And speak off half a dozen dang'rous words
How they might hurt their enemies, if they durst,
And this is all.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: But brother Anthony—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Come, ’tis no matter.
Do not you meddle. Let me deal in this.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Gentlemen both, we will not wake your patience.
My heart is sorry for your daughter’s death,
But, on my honor, she was charged with nothing
But what was true and very full of proof.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: My lord, my lord—

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: I will not hear you.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: No? Come, brother; away! I will be heard.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: And shall, or some of us will smart for it.
Exeunt LEONATO and ANTONIO
Enter BENEDICK

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: See, see, here comes the man we went to seek.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Now, Signior, what news?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: [to DON PEDRO] Good day, my lord.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Welcome, Signior. You are almost come to part almost
a fray.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
We had like to have had our two noses snapped off with
two old men without teeth.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Leonato and his brother. What think’st thou? Had
we fought, I doubt we should have been too young for
them.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
In a false quarrel there is no true valor. I came to
seek you both.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
We have been up and down to seek thee, for we are
high-proof melancholy and would fain have it beaten
away. Wilt thou use thy wit?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: It is in my scabbard. Shall I draw it?

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Dost thou wear thy wit by thy side?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Never any did so, though very many have been beside
their wit. I will bid thee draw, as we do the minstrels:
draw to pleasure us.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
As I am an honest man, he looks pale.—Art thou sick,
or angry?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
[to BENEDICK] What, courage, man! What though
care killed a cat? Thou hast mettle enough in thee to
kill care.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Sir, I shall meet your wit in the career, an you charge
it
against me. I pray you, choose another subject.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
[to DON PEDRO] Nay, then, give him another staff. This
last was broke 'cross.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
By this light, he changes more and more. I think he be
angry indeed.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: If he be, he knows how to turn his girdle.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Shall I speak a word in your ear?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: God bless me from a challenge!

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
[aside to CLAUDIO ] You are a villain. I jest not. I
will make it good how you dare, with what you dare, and
when you dare. Do me right, or I will protest your
cowardice. You have killed a sweet lady, and her death
shall fall heavy on you. Let me hear from you.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Well, I will meet you, so I may have good cheer.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: What, a feast, a feast?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
I' faith, I thank him. He hath bid me to a calf’s head
and a capon, the which if I do not carve most
curiously, say my knife’s naught. Shall I not find a
woodcock too?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Sir, your wit ambles well; it goes easily.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
I’ll tell thee how Beatrice praised thy wit he other
day. I said thou hadst a fine wit. “True,” said she, “a
fine little one.” “No,” said I, “a great wit.” “Right,”
says she, “a great gross one.” “Nay,” said I, “a good
wit.” “Just,” said she, “it hurts nobody.” “Nay,” said
I, “the gentleman is wise.” “Certain,” said she, “a wise
gentleman.” “Nay,” said I, “he hath the tongues.” “That
I believe,” said she, “for he swore a thing to me on
Monday night, which he forswore on Tuesday morning;
there’s a double tongue, there’s two tongues.” Thus did
she an hour together transshape thy particular virtues.
Yet at last she concluded with a sigh, thou wast the
proper’st man in Italy.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
For the which she wept heartily and said she cared
not.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Yea, that she did. But yet for all that, an if she did
not hate him deadly, she would love him dearly. The old
man’s daughter told us all.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
All, all. And, moreover, God saw him when he was hid
in the garden.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
But when shall we set the savage bull’s horns on the
sensible Benedick’s head?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Yea, and text underneath: “Here dwells Benedick
the married man?”

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Fare you well, boy. You know my mind. I will leave you
now to your gossip-like humor. You break jests as
braggarts do their blades, which, God be thanked, hurt
not. —My lord, for your many courtesies I thank you. I
must discontinue your company. Your brother the Bastard
is fled from Messina. You have among you killed a sweet
and innocent lady. For my Lord Lackbeard there, he and I
shall meet, and till then peace be with him.
Exit

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: He is in earnest.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
In most profound earnest, and, I’ll warrant you, for
the love of Beatrice.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: And hath challenged thee?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Most sincerely.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
What a pretty thing man is when he goes in his doublet
and hose and leaves off his wit!

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
He is then a giant to an ape; but then is an ape a
doctor to such a man.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
But soft you, let me be. Pluck up, my heart, and be
sad. Did he not say my brother was fled?
Enter DOGBERRY,VERGES, and the Watch, with CONRAD and BORACHIO

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Come you, sir. If justice cannot tame you, she shall
ne'er weigh more reasons in her balance. Nay, an you be
a cursing hypocrite once, you must be looked to.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: How now? Two of my brother’s men bound! Borachio one!

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Hearken after their offense, my lord.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Officers, what offense have these men done?

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
DON PEDRO
units.text:
First, I ask thee what they have done; thirdly, I ask
thee what’s their offense; sixth and lastly, why they
are committed; and, to conclude, what you lay to their
charge.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Rightly reasoned, and in his own division; and, by my
troth, there’s one meaning well suited.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
[to BORACHIO and CONRAD ] Who have you
offended, masters, that you are thus bound to your
answer? This learned constable is too cunning to be
understood. What’s your offense?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text:
Sweet Prince, let me go no farther to mine answer. Do
you hear me, and let this count kill me. I have deceived
even your very eyes. What your wisdoms could not
discover, these shallow fools have brought to light, who
in the night overheard me confessing to this man how
Don John your brother incensed me to slander the Lady
Hero, how you were brought into the orchard and saw me
court Margaret in Hero’s garments, how you disgraced her
when you should marry her. My villainy they have upon
record, which I had rather seal with my death than
repeat over to my shame. The lady is dead upon mine and
my master’s false accusation. And, briefly, I desire
nothing but the reward of a villain.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
[to CLAUDIO] Runs not this speech like iron through
your blood?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: I have drunk poison whiles he uttered it.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: [to BORACHIO] But did my brother set thee on to this?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: Yea, and paid me richly for the practice of it.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
He is composed and framed of treachery,
And fled he is upon this villainy.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
Sweet Hero, now thy image doth appear
In the rare semblance that I loved it first.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Come, bring away the plaintiffs. By this time our
sexton hath reformed Signior Leonato of the matter.
And, masters, do not forget to specify, when time and
place shall serve, that I am an ass.

units.speaker: VERGES
units.text:
Here, here comes Master Signior Leonato, and the
Sexton too.
Enter LEONATO and ANTONIO, with the SEXTON

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Which is the villain? Let me see his eyes,
That, when I note another man like him,
I may avoid him. Which of these is he?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: If you would know your wronger, look on me.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Art thou the slave that with thy breath hast killed
Mine innocent child?

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text: Yea, even I alone.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
No, not so, villain, thou beliest thyself.
Here stand a pair of honorable men—
A third is fled—that had a hand in it.—
I thank you, princes, for my daughter’s death.
Record it with your high and worthy deeds.
'Twas bravely done, if you bethink you of it.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
I know not how to pray your patience,
Yet I must speak. Choose your revenge yourself.
Impose me to what penance your invention
Can lay upon my sin. Yet sinned I not
But in mistaking.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
By my soul, nor I,
And yet to satisfy this good old man
I would bend under any heavy weight
That he’ll enjoin me to.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
I cannot bid you bid my daughter live—
That were impossible —but, I pray you both,
Possess the people in Messina here
How innocent she died. And if your love
Can labor ought in sad invention,
Hang her an epitaph upon her tomb
And sing it to her bones. Sing it tonight.
Tomorrow morning come you to my house,
And since you could not be my son-in-law,
Be yet my nephew. My brother hath a daughter,
Almost the copy of my child that’s dead,
And she alone is heir to both of us.
Give her the right you should have given her cousin,
And so dies my revenge.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
O noble sir!
Your overkindness doth wring tears from me.
I do embrace your offer; and dispose
For henceforth of poor Claudio.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Tomorrow then I will expect your coming.
Tonight I take my leave. This naughty man
Shall face to face be brought to Margaret,
Who I believe was packed in all this wrong,
Hired to it by your brother.

units.speaker: BORACHIO
units.text:
No, by my soul, she was not,
Nor knew not what she did when she spoke to me,
But always hath been just and virtuous
In any thing that I do know by her.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
[to LEONATO ] Moreover, sir, which indeed is not
under white and black, this plaintiff here, the
offender, did call me ass. I beseech you, let it be
remembered in his punishment. And also the watch heard
them talk of one Deformed. They say he wears a key in
his ear and a lock hanging by it and borrows money in
God’s name, the which he hath used so long and never
paid that now men grow hard-hearted and will lend
nothing for God’s sake. Pray you, examine him upon that
point.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: I thank thee for thy care and honest pains.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
Your worship speaks like a most thankful and
reverent youth, and I praise God for you.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: [giving him money] There’s for thy pains.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text: God save the foundation!

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Go, I discharge thee of thy prisoner, and I thank thee.

units.speaker: DOGBERRY
units.text:
I leave an arrant knave with your Worship, which I
beseech your Worship to correct yourself, for the
example of others. God keep your Worship! I wish your
Worship well. God restore you to health! I humbly give
you leave to depart, and
if a merry meeting may be wished, God prohibit it!—
Come, neighbor.
Exeunt DOGBERRY and VERGES

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Until tomorrow morning, lords, farewell.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Farewell, my lords. We look for you tomorrow.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: We will not fail.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Tonight I’ll mourn with Hero.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
[to the Watch]
Bring you these fellows on.—We’ll talk with Margaret,
How her acquaintance grew with this lewd fellow.
Exeunt
Enter BENEDICK and MARGARET

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Pray thee, sweet Mistress Margaret, deserve well at
my hands by helping me to the speech of Beatrice.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: Will you then write me a sonnet in praise of my beauty?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
In so high a style, Margaret, that no man living shall
come over it, for in most comely truth thou deservest
it.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
To have no man come over me! Why, shall I always keep
below stairs?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Thy wit is as quick as the greyhound’s mouth; it
catches.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
And yours as blunt as the fencer’s foils, which hit but
hurt not.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
A most manly wit, Margaret, it will not hurt a woman.
And so, I pray thee, call Beatrice. I give thee the
bucklers.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text: Give us the swords; we have bucklers of our own.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
If you use them, Margaret, you must put in the pikes
with a vice, and they are dangerous weapons for maids.

units.speaker: MARGARET
units.text:
Well, I will call Beatrice to you, who I think hath
legs.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: And therefore will come.
Exit MARGARET

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
[sings]
The god of love ,
That sits above ,
And knows me, and knows me ,
How pitiful I deserve —
I mean in singing. But in loving, Leander the good
swimmer, Troilus the first employer of panders, and a
whole bookful of these quondam carpetmongers, whose
names yet run smoothly in the even road of a blank
verse, why, they were never so truly turned over and
over as my poor self in love. Marry, I cannot show it in
rhyme. I have tried. I can find out no rhyme to “lady”
but “baby”—an innocent rhyme; for “scorn,” “horn”—a hard
rhyme; for, “school,” “fool”—a babbling rhyme; very
ominous endings. No, I was not born under a rhyming
planet, nor I cannot woo in festival terms.
Enter BEATRICE

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Sweet Beatrice, wouldst thou come when I called thee?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Yea, Signior, and depart when you bid me.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Oh, stay but till then!

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
“Then” is spoken. Fare you well now. And yet, ere I go,
let me go with that I came, which is, with knowing what
hath passed between you and Claudio.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Only foul words, and thereupon I will kiss thee.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Foul words is but foul wind, and foul wind is but
foul breath, and foul breath is noisome. Therefore I
will depart unkissed.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Thou hast frighted the word out of his right sense,
so forcible is thy wit. But I must tell thee plainly,
Claudio undergoes my challenge, and either I must
shortly hear from him, or I will subscribe him a coward.
And I pray thee now tell me, for which of my bad parts
didst thou first fall in love with me?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
For them all together, which maintained so politic a
state of evil that they will not admit any good part to
intermingle with them. But for which of my good parts
did you first suffer love for me?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Suffer love! A good epithet! I do suffer love indeed,
for I love thee against my will.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
In spite of your heart, I think. Alas, poor heart, if
you spite it for my sake, I will spite it for yours, for
I will never love that which my friend hates.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Thou and I are too wise to woo peaceably.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
It appears not in this confession. There’s not one wise
man among twenty that will praise himself.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
An old, an old instance, Beatrice, that lived in the
time of good neighbors. If a man do not erect in this
age his own tomb ere he dies, he shall live no longer in
monument than
the bell rings and the widow weeps.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: And how long is that, think you?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Question: why, an hour in clamor and a quarter in
rheum. Therefore is it most expedient for the wise, if
Don Worm, his conscience, find no impediment to the
contrary, to be the trumpet of his own virtues, as I am
to myself. So much for praising myself, who, I myself
will bear witness, is praiseworthy. An now tell me, how
doth your cousin?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Very ill.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: And how do you?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Very ill, too.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Serve God, love me, and mend. There will I leave you
too, for here comes one in haste.
Enter URSULA

units.speaker: URSULA
units.text:
Madam, you must come to your uncle. Yonder’s old coil
at home. It is proved my Lady Hero hath been
falsely accused, the Prince and Claudio mightily abused,
and Don John is the author of all, who is fled and
gone. Will you come presently?
Exit

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Will you go hear this news, Signior?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
I will live in thy heart, die in thy lap, and be buried
in thy eyes—and moreover, I will go with thee to thy
uncle’s.
Exeunt
Enter DON PEDRO, CLAUDIO, three or four LORDS with tapers, and musicians

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Is this the monument of Leonato?

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text: It is, my lord.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
[reading an epitaph]
Done to death by slanderous tongues
Was the Hero that here lies.
Death, in guerdon of her wrongs,
Gives her fame which never dies.
So the life that died with shame
Lives in death with glorious fame.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Good morrow, masters. Put your torches out.
The wolves have preyed, and look, the gentle day,
Before the wheels of Phoebus, round about
Dapples the drowsy east with spots of grey.
Thanks to you all, and leave us. Fare you well.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Good morrow, masters. Each his several way.
Exeunt LORDS and Musicians

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Come, let us hence, and put on other weeds,
And then to Leonato’s we will go.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
And Hymen now with luckier issue speed ’s
Than this for whom we rendered up this woe.
Exeunt
Enter LEONATO, ANTONIO, BENEDICK, BEATRICE, MARGARET, URSULA, FRIAR FRANCIS, and HERO

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: Did I not tell you she was innocent?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
So are the Prince and Claudio, who accused her
Upon the error that you heard debated.
But Margaret was in some fault for this,
Although against her will, as it appears
In the true course of all the question.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Well, I am glad that all things sort so well.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
And so am I, being else by faith enforced
To call young Claudio to a reckoning for it.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Well, daughter, and you gentlewomen all,
Withdraw into a chamber by yourselves,
And when I send for you, come hither masked.
The Prince and Claudio promised by this hour
To visit me. —You know your office, brother.
You must be father to your brother’s daughter,
And give her to young Claudio.
Exeunt Ladies

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Which I will do with confirmed countenance.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Friar, I must entreat your pains, I think.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text: To do what, Signior?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
To bind me or undo me, one of them.—
Signior Leonato, truth it is, good Signior,
Your niece regards me with an eye of favor.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: That eye my daughter lent her; ’tis most true.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: And I do with an eye of love requite her.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
The sight whereof I think you had from me,
From Claudio and the Prince. But what’s your will?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Your answer, sir, is enigmatical.
But for my will, my will is your goodwill
May stand with ours, this day to be conjoined
In the state of honorable marriage—
In which, good Friar, I shall desire your help.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: My heart is with your liking.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
And my help.
Here comes the Prince and Claudio.
Enter DON PEDRO and CLAUDIO, and two or three others

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: Good morrow to this fair assembly.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
Good morrow, Prince; good morrow, Claudio.
We here attend you. Are you yet determined
Today to marry with my brother’s daughter?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: I’ll hold my mind were she an Ethiope.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Call her forth, brother. Here’s the friar ready.
Exit ANTONIO

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text:
Good morrow, Benedick. Why, what’s the matter
That you have such a February face,
So full of frost, of storm and cloudiness?

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
I think he thinks upon the savage bull.
Tush, fear not, man. We’ll tip thy horns with gold,
And all Europa shall rejoice at thee
As once Europa did at lusty Jove
When he would play the noble beast in love.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Bull Jove, sir, had an amiable low,
And some such strange bull leapt your father’s cow
And got a calf in that same noble feat
Much like to you, for you have just his bleat.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: For this I owe you. Here comes other reck'nings.
Enter ANTONIO, HERO, BEATRICE, MARGARET, URSULA, the ladies masked

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Which is the lady I must seize upon?

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: This same is she, and I do give you her.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Why, then she’s mine.—Sweet, let me see your face.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text:
No, that you shall not till you take her hand
Before this friar and swear to marry her.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
[to HERO] Give me your hand before this holy friar.
I am your husband, if you like of me.

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
And when I lived, I was your other wife,
And when you loved, you were my other husband.
[she unmasks]

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text: Another Hero!

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
Nothing certainer.
One Hero died defiled, but I do live,
And surely as I live, I am a maid.

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: The former Hero! Hero that is dead!

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: She died, my lord, but whiles her slander lived.

units.speaker: FRIAR FRANCIS
units.text:
All this amazement can I qualify
When after that the holy rites are ended
I’ll tell you largely of fair Hero’s death.
Meantime let wonder seem familiar,
And to the chapel let us presently.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Soft and fair, Friar.—Which is Beatrice?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: [unmasking] I answer to that name. What is your will?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Do not you love me?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Why no, no more than reason.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Why then, your uncle and the Prince and Claudio
Have been deceived. They swore you did.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: Do not you love me?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Troth, no, no more than reason.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
Why then, my cousin, Margaret, and Ursula
Are much deceived, for they did swear you did.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: They swore that you were almost sick for me.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: They swore that you were well-nigh dead for me.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: 'Tis no such matter. Then you do not love me?

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text: No, truly, but in friendly recompense.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: Come, cousin, I am sure you love the gentleman.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
And I’ll be sworn upon ’t that he loves her,
For here’s a paper written in his hand,
A halting sonnet of his own pure brain,
Fashioned to Beatrice.
[shows a paper]

units.speaker: HERO
units.text:
And here’s another,
Writ in my cousin’s hand, stol'n from her pocket,
Containing her affection unto Benedick.
[shows a paper]

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
A miracle! Here’s our own hands against our hearts.
Come, I will have thee, but, by this light, I take thee
for pity.

units.speaker: BEATRICE
units.text:
I would not deny you, but, by this good day, I yield
upon great persuasion, and partly to save your life, for
I was told you were in a consumption.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text: Peace! I will stop your mouth.
They kiss

units.speaker: DON PEDRO
units.text: How dost thou, Benedick, the married man?

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
I’ll tell thee what, Prince: a college of wit-crackers
cannot flout me out of my humor. Dost thou think I care
for a satire or an epigram? No. If a man will be beaten
with brains, he shall wear nothing handsome about him.
In brief, since I do purpose to marry, I will think
nothing to any purpose that the world can say against
it, and therefore never flout at me for what I have said
against it. For man is a giddy thing, and this is my
conclusion. —For thy part, Claudio, I did think to have
beaten thee, but in that thou art like to be my
kinsman, live unbruised, and love my cousin.

units.speaker: CLAUDIO
units.text:
I had well hoped thou wouldst have denied Beatrice,
that I might have cudgeled thee out of thy single life,
to make thee a double-dealer, which out of question,
thou wilt be, if my cousin do not look exceedingly
narrowly to thee.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
Come, come, we are friends. Let’s have a dance ere we
are married, that we may lighten our own hearts and our
wives' heels.

units.speaker: LEONATO
units.text: We’ll have dancing afterward.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
First, of my word! Therefore play, music.—Prince, thou
art sad. Get thee a wife, get thee a wife. There is no
staff more reverend than one tipped with horn.
Enter a MESSENGER

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
[to DON PEDRO] My lord, your brother John is ta'en in
flight
And brought with armed men back to Messina.

units.speaker: BENEDICK
units.text:
[to DON PEDRO] Think not on him till tomorrow. I’ll
devise thee brave punishments for him.—Strike up,
pipers.
Exeunt

sentences.text: Enter LEONATO, Governor of Messina; HERO, his daughter; and BEATRICE his niece, with a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
I learn in this letter that Don Pedro of Aragon comes
this night to Messina.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
He is very near by this. He was not three leagues off
when I left him.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
He is very near by this. He was not three leagues off
when I left him.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: How many gentlemen have you lost in this action?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: But few of any sort, and none of name.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
A victory is twice itself when the achiever brings home
full numbers. I find here that Don Pedro hath bestowed
much
honor on a young Florentine called Claudio.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
A victory is twice itself when the achiever brings home
full numbers. I find here that Don Pedro hath bestowed
much
honor on a young Florentine called Claudio.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Much deserved on his part, and equally remembered
by Don Pedro. He hath borne himself beyond the promise
of his age, doing in the figure of a lamb the feats of a
lion. He hath indeed better bettered expectation than
you must expect of me to tell you how.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
He hath an uncle here in Messina will be very much
glad of it.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
I have already delivered him letters, and there
appears much joy in him—even so much that joy could not
show itself modest enough without a badge of bitterness.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
I have already delivered him letters, and there
appears much joy in him—even so much that joy could not
show itself modest enough without a badge of bitterness.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: Did he break out into tears?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: In great measure.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
A kind overflow of kindness. There are no faces truer
than those that are so washed. How much better is it to
weep at joy than to joy at weeping!

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
I pray you, is Signor Montanto returned from the wars
or no?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
I know none of that name, lady. There was none such in
the army of any sort.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
I know none of that name, lady. There was none such in
the army of any sort.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: What is he that you ask for, niece?

sentences.speaker: HERO
sentences.text: My cousin means Signor Benedick of Padua.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Oh, he’s returned, and as pleasant as ever he was.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
He set up his bills here in Messina and challenged
Cupid at the flight, and my uncle’s Fool, reading the
challenge, subscribed for Cupid and challenged him at
the bird-bolt. I pray you, how many hath he killed and
eaten in these wars? But how many hath he killed? For
indeed I promised to eat all of his killing.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
Faith, niece, you tax Signor Benedick too much, but
he’ll be meet with you, I doubt it not.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:

he’ll be meet with you, I doubt it not.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: He hath done good service, lady, in these wars.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
You had musty victual, and he hath holp to eat it. He
is a very valiant trencherman. He hath an excellent
stomach.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: And a good soldier too, lady.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: And a good soldier too, lady.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: And a good soldier to a lady, but what is he to a lord?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
A lord to a lord, a man to a man, stuffed with all
honorable virtues.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
A lord to a lord, a man to a man, stuffed with all
honorable virtues.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
It is so indeed. He is no less than a stuffed man. But
for the stuffing—well, we are all mortal.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
You must not, sir, mistake my niece. There is a kind
of merry war betwixt Signor Benedick and her. They
never meet but there’s a skirmish of wit between them.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
Alas, he gets nothing by that. In our last conflict
four of his five wits went halting off, and now is the
whole man governed with one, so that if he have wit
enough to keep himself warm, let him bear it for a
difference between himself and his horse, for it is all
the wealth that he hath left to be known a reasonable
creature. Who is his companion now? He hath every month
a new sworn brother.

sentences.text: Is ’t possible?

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
Very easily possible. He wears his faith but as the
fashion of his hat; it ever changes with the next block.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: I see, lady, the gentleman is not in your books.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
No. An he were, I would burn my study. But I pray
you, who is his companion? Is there no young squarer now
that will make a voyage with him to the devil?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: He is most in the company of the right noble Claudio.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
O Lord, he will hang upon him like a disease! He is
sooner caught than the pestilence, and the taker runs
presently mad. God help the noble Claudio! If he have
caught the Benedick, it will cost him a thousand pound
ere a be cured.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: I will hold friends with you, lady.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: Do, good friend.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: You will never run mad, niece.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: No, not till a hot January.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Don Pedro is approached.
Enter DON PEDRO, Prince of Aragon, with CLAUDIO, BENEDICK, BALTHASAR, and DON JOHN the bastard

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text:
Good Signor Leonato, are you come to meet your
trouble? The fashion of the world is to avoid cost, and
you encounter it.

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text:
Good Signor Leonato, are you come to meet your
trouble? The fashion of the world is to avoid cost, and
you encounter it.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
Never came trouble to my house in the likeness of
your Grace, for trouble being gone, comfort should
remain, but
when you depart from me, sorrow abides and happiness
takes his leave.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:

when you depart from me, sorrow abides and happiness
takes his leave.

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text:
You embrace your charge too willingly. I think this is
your daughter.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: Her mother hath many times told me so.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: Were you in doubt, sir, that you asked her?

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: Signor Benedick, no, for then were you a child.

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text:
You have it full, Benedick. We may guess by this what
you are, being a man. Truly, the lady fathers
herself.—Be happy, lady, for you are like an honorable
father.

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO

LEONATO and DON PEDRO move to one side, still talking

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
If Signor Leonato be her father, she would not have
his head on her shoulders for all Messina, as like him
as she is.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
I wonder that you will still be talking, Signor
Benedick. Nobody marks you.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
I wonder that you will still be talking, Signor
Benedick. Nobody marks you.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: What, my dear Lady Disdain! Are you yet living?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: What, my dear Lady Disdain! Are you yet living?

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
Is it possible disdain should die while she hath such
meet food to feed it as Signor Benedick? Courtesy itself
must convert to disdain if you come in her presence.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Then is courtesy a turncoat. But it is certain I am
loved of all ladies, only you excepted. And I would I
could find in my heart that I had not a hard heart, for
truly I love none.

sentences.text:
A dear happiness to women. They would else have
been troubled with a pernicious suitor. I thank God and
my cold blood I am of your humor for that. I had rather
hear my dog bark at a crow than a man swear he loves me.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK

God keep your Ladyship still in that mind, so some
gentle-man or other shall ’scape a predestinate
scratched face.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text:
Scratching could not make it worse an ’twere such a
face as yours were.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: Well, you are a rare parrot-teacher.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: A bird of my tongue is better than a beast of yours.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
I would my horse had the speed of your tongue and so
good a continuer. But keep your way, i' God’s name. I
have done.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:

have done.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: You always end with a jade’s trick. I know you of old.

sentences.speaker: BEATRICE
sentences.text: You always end with a jade’s trick. I know you of old.
LEONATO and DON PEDRO come forward

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text:
That is the sum of all, Leonato. —Signior Claudio
and Signior Benedick, my dear friend Leonato hath
invited you all. I tell him we shall stay here at the
least a month, and he heartily prays some occasion may
detain us longer. I dare
swear he is no hypocrite but prays from his heart.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
If you swear, my lord, you shall not be forsworn. [to
DON JOHN] Let me bid you welcome, my lord. Being
reconciled to the Prince your brother, I owe you all
duty.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text:
If you swear, my lord, you shall not be forsworn. [to

reconciled to the Prince your brother, I owe you all
duty.

sentences.speaker: DON JOHN
sentences.text: I thank you. I am not of many words, but I thank you.

sentences.speaker: DON JOHN
sentences.text: I thank you. I am not of many words, but I thank you.

sentences.speaker: LEONATO
sentences.text: Please it your Grace lead on?

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text: Your hand, Leonato. We will go together.

sentences.speaker: DON PEDRO
sentences.text: Your hand, Leonato. We will go together.
Exeunt. Manent BENEDICK and CLAUDIO

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
Benedick, didst thou note the daughter of Signior
Leonato?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: I noted her not, but I looked on her.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text: Is she not a modest young lady?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Do you question me as an honest man should do, for
my simple true judgment? Or would you have me speak
after my custom, as being a professed tyrant to their
sex?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Do you question me as an honest man should do, for
my simple true judgment? Or would you have me speak
after my custom, as being a professed tyrant to their
sex?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text: No, I pray thee speak in sober judgment.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Why, i' faith, methinks she’s too low for a high
praise, too brown for a fair praise, and too little for
a great praise. Only this commendation I can afford her,
that were she other than she is, she were unhandsome,
and being no other but as she is, I do not like her.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
Thou thinkest I am in sport. I pray thee tell me truly
how thou lik’st her.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:

how thou lik’st her.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text: Would you buy her, that you enquire after her?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text: Can the world buy such a jewel?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Yea, and a case to put it into. But speak you this with
a sad brow? Or do you play the flouting jack, to tell
us Cupid is a good hare-finder and Vulcan a rare
carpenter? Come, in what key shall a man take you to go
in the song?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Yea, and a case to put it into. But speak you this with
a sad brow? Or do you play the flouting jack, to tell
us Cupid is a good hare-finder and Vulcan a rare
carpenter? Come, in what key shall a man take you to go
in the song?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
In mine eye she is the sweetest lady that ever I looked
on.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
I can see yet without spectacles, and I see no such
matter. There’s her cousin, an she were not possessed
with a fury, exceeds her as much in beauty as the first
of May doth the last of December. But I hope you have no
intent to turn husband, have you?

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
I would scarce trust myself, though I had sworn
the contrary, if Hero would be my wife.

sentences.speaker: BENEDICK
sentences.text:
Is ’t come to this? In faith, hath not the world one
man but he will wear his cap with suspicion? Shall I
never see a bachelor of three-score again? Go to, i'
faith, an thou wilt needs thrust thy neck into a yoke,
wear the print of it, and sigh away Sundays. Look, Don
Pedro is returned to seek you.
